text.title: Viral Metrics and K-factor Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Viral Metrics and K-factor` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Canonical K-factor formula MUST be K = i * c where i = avg invites sent per user, c = invitee-to-active conversion rate. Other formulations (e.g. invite-to-signup) are derivatives and must be labeled.

rationale.source: Andrew Chen viral coefficient definition; Sequoia growth math

rule.rationale: Mixed definitions across reports yield uncomparable numbers. K = i * c is the canonical formulation; everything else is a labeled variant.

rule.statement: K-factor MUST be measured weekly (cohort-based), not as cumulative all-time. Cumulative K averages dead loops with active ones.
rule.rationale: Cumulative K hides decay. Weekly cohort K shows whether loop is gaining or losing momentum — actionable signal.

rule.statement: Report MUST surface i and c separately. Combined K alone hides which leg is the bottleneck.
rule.rationale: K = 0.2 from i=2, c=10% has different fix than K = 0.2 from i=0.5, c=40%. Decomposition tells team which leg to optimize.

rule.statement: Viral cycle time (mean days from user activation → invite sent → invitee active) MUST be measured. Long cycle times mute compound growth even with high K.

rationale.source: Adam Penenberg Viral Loop; cycle-time math

rule.rationale: K=0.5 + 7-day cycle = different growth than K=0.5 + 30-day cycle. Cycle time multiplies the loop power; tracking it surfaces the silent bottleneck.

rule.statement: Effective K MUST adjust for retention: K_eff = K * D30_retention. Report both raw K and K_eff. Raw K alone overstates loop value on leaky-bucket products.
rule.rationale: Raw K on a 15% D30 product is theatre — invitees vanish. K_eff = K * retention shows real compounding. Decision-maker needs both.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
